title: e2e: clean up kube, to avoid error in teardown
body:
Minor. Found by temporarily enabling #18442.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Oops, forgot to link to [the error itself](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5684977854054400/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-container-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-podman-play-kube-test-with-reserved-volumes-from-annotation-in-yaml--1).

And, this PR [does not fix it](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4852164502224896/html/int-podman-fedora-37-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-podman-play-kube-test-with-reserved-volumes-from-annotation-in-yaml--1), so, closing.

And, reopening, because this *does* fix it, at least, moves the error message from teardown (where it would be fatal if #18442 could ever merge) to the test itself, where it's noisy but harmless. Fixing the actual problem, so the error doesn't happen at all, is left as an exercise for someone more kube-savvy.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This reproduces trivially on the command line:
```console
$ bin/podman create --name ctr1 -v $(pwd)/vvv $IMAGE
<CTR1SHA>
$ bin/podman create --volumes-from ctr1 --name ctr2 $IMAGE
<CTR2SHA>
$ bin/podman kube generate --podman-only -f foo.yml ctr2
$ bin/podman kube play foo.yml
Pod:
<PODSHA>
Container:
<ONEMORESHA>

$ bin/podman kube down foo.yml
ERRO[0000] Removing volume <VOLSHA>: volume <VOLSHA> is being used by the following container(s): <CTR1SHA>, <CTR2SHA>: volume is being used 
Pods stopped:
<PODSHA>
Pods removed:
<PODSHA>
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
```
Exit status is 0 despite the `ERRO`. This squicks me out, I'd prefer WARN, but, shrug.

Since this looks deliberate, I've re-pushed with an explicit check for the error message.
I agree this should be a warning not an error.  Lets open a different issue and fix there.

/hold

Leaving this open, since I assume the message will remain regardless (at different severity)

If the volume is still being used we should just ignore that. It makes no sense to print a warning or error in such a case. IMO the volume should only be removed if nothing else depends on that.
cc @mheon

I can see the case for a warning. It's a resource created by `kube play` that is not being torn down properly by `kube down`; warning the user that we failed to remove all resources created seems sensible.

But the volume was created when ctr1 was created so play kube just reuses that. I don't think it is sane to attempt to remove volumes that are still in use by others containers, i.e. ctr1 and ctr2 in this case. If ctr1/2 were removed then I agree the volume should be removed.
Sure, not disagreeing, I just feel it's worth warning the user that we tried to remove a volume, but it was still in use.
Shouldn't the only problem here be if a third container outside of podman kube play got started and used the volume?
What I'm hearing in this discussion is that #18442 would be *really* nice to have enabled in CI. Some of us already know this; I hope the conversation is swaying others.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@edsantiago what should we do with this PR?

From the discussion, I get the sense that someone should file an issue to ... I have no idea. @mheon, @Luap99, you seem to have the strongest ideas about what should happen here. (My strong idea is that there should be no warning whatsoever, but I might be in the minority). Could one of you please file an issue with the appropriate details? We can then close this PR.
IMO there should be no warning in such case as this is expected for such configurations. I can open a PR for that if others agree.

> IMO there should be no warning in such case as this is expected for such configurations. I can open a PR for that if others agree.

SGTM :+1:
